steps.title: Identify the y-intercept location
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis. This always occurs at $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-value directly from the graph
steps.content: At $x = 0$ on the exponential curve, trace vertically to find where the graph intersects the y-axis. The curve passes through the point $(0, 3)$, so $y = 3$.
